titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Emery Huston Papers Relating to the 1920 Pikes Peak Hill Climb
Huston (Emery) Papers Relating to the 1920 Pikes Peak Hill Climb
titlestmt.author: Matthew Daniel Mason and Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 15881054.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday July 8, 2022 at 03:01

did.unittitle: Emery Huston papers relating to the 1920 Pikes Peak Hill Climb

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Photograph album, scrapbook, and newspaper clippings collected by Joseph Emery Huston primarily documenting the use of Lexington automobiles in the 1920 Pikes Peak Hill Climb in Colorado. Materials relate to the Lexington Motor Company, the hillclimb, and drivers Otto Loesche and Al Cline. Some materials relate to the Ansted family.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James Arsenault & Company on the William Robertson Coe Fund No. 3, 2012.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Photographs, 1920-1957. II. Scrapbook, 1896-1919. III. Newspaper Clippings, 1914-1980.

bioghist.head: Emery Huston
bioghist.p: Following his graduation from Wabash College in 1913, Joseph Emery Huston (1891-1951) of Connersville, Indiana, became an advertising and assistant sales manager of the Lexington-Howard Company, manufacturers of the Lexington automobile.

Huston married Nelle Ansted (1890-1974) in 1914. Ansted's father, Edward Willard Ansted (1852-1917), had acquired the Lexington Motor Company in 1913. Edward Ansted's son, Frank B. Ansted (1884-1933), served as President of the company with Emery Huston as Vice President.

bioghist.head: Pikes Peak Auto Hill Climb
bioghist.p: The first Pikes Peak Auto Hill Climb (later known as the Pikes Peak International Hill Climb or The Race to the Clouds) took place in 1916 to publicize American entrepreneur and philanthropist Spencer Penrose's creation of the Pikes Peak Highway in Colorado. The second race took place in 1920, following World War I.

bioghist.head: Lexington Motor Company
bioghist.p: The Lexington Motor Company was founded in Lexington, Kentucky, in 1909. The Lexington automobile was manufactured in Connersville, Indiana, from 1910 to 1927. The company built two short-wheelbase race cars with Ansted engines for the 1920 Pikes Peak Hill Climb. The cars placed first and second with drivers Otto Loesche and Al Cline, respectively.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Emery Huston Papers Relating to the 1920 Pikes Peak Hill Climb. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph album, scrapbook, and newspaper clippings collected by Joseph Emery Huston primarily documenting the use of Lexington automobiles in the 1920 Pikes Peak Hill Climb in Colorado. Materials relate to the Lexington Motor Company, the hillclimb, and drivers Otto Loesche and Al Cline. Some materials relate to the Ansted family.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Emery Huston Papers Relating to the 1920 Pikes Peak Hill Climb is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

subject.part: Pikes Peak Race to the Clouds

did.unittitle: Photographs

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged chronologically following the album.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I consists of a photograph album with loose materials removed from the album. Photographs are primarily of the two Lexington automobiles--with drivers Otto Loesche and Al Cline--that placed first and second in the Pikes Peak Hill Climb on 1920 September 8. Included is an undated lantern slide portrait of Emery Huston.

did.unittitle: Photograph album
Hubbard, Elbert, "A Little Journey to Connersville"
Statement of account with Lexington Motor Company
Revery poem dedicated to Edward Ansted Huston
Lewis, Reverend W. D. "The Anthem of Industry"
Thank you letter from the Ansted family
Lexington Motor Company, "The Personal Side of a Factory Family"
Letter from William B. Ansted, Jr. to Joseph E. Huston
Huston, Emery, "Journey's End"
Lantern slide portrait of Emery Huston
Scrapbook

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is not arranged in any particular order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II consists of a scrapbook (now empty) and miscellaneous newspaper clippings and correspondence (removed from scrapbook).

did.unittitle: Scrapbook (empty)
"In Society - Bridge Luncheon"
Coolidge, Dr. Mary Roberts, "Ladies in the Making"

"Christmas and its Meaning"

"Monday Bridge"
"Girls' Glee Club at South Central High School"
"Sues for Large Amount"
"The Thiebaud Scholarship"

"Quincy A. Mount Suddenly Called"
Newspaper Clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III consists of newspaper clippings relating to the 1920 Pikes Peak Hill Climb and the Lexington Motor Company.
